steps.title: Recognize the quadratic formula structure
steps.content: The expression $x = \frac{-7 + \sqrt{k}}{2}$ matches the quadratic formula pattern $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}$. From $x^2 + 7x + 5 = 0$, we identify $a = 1$, $b = 7$, and $c = 5$.

steps.title: Calculate the discriminant to find k
steps.content:
Compute the expression under the radical:
$$b^2 - 4ac = 7^2 - 4(1)(5) = 49 - 20 = 29$$
In the given form $x = \frac{-7 + \sqrt{k}}{2}$, this means $\sqrt{k} = \sqrt{29}$, so $k = 29$.
